# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build and Notification

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
      
      - name: Sanitize PR title
        id: sanitize
        run: |
          PR_TITLE=$(jq -r '.pull_request.title' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          PR_BODY=$(jq -r '.pull_request.body // ""' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          
          # Sanitize by removing potentially dangerous characters
          SAFE_TITLE=$(echo "$PR_TITLE" | tr -cd '[:alnum:][:space:]._-' | cut -c1-100)
          SAFE_BODY=$(echo "$PR_BODY" | tr -cd '[:alnum:][:space:]._-' | cut -c1-500)
          
          # Use environment files instead of set-output
          echo "pr_title=$SAFE_TITLE" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "pr_body=$SAFE_BODY" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          
          # Store in environment file for multi-line content
          {
            echo "SAFE_PR_TITLE<<EOF"
            echo "$SAFE_TITLE"
            echo "EOF"
            echo "SAFE_PR_BODY<<EOF"
            echo "$SAFE_BODY"
            echo "EOF"
          } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
      
      - name: Build application
        run: |
          echo "Building application for PR: ${{ env.SAFE_PR_TITLE }}"
          # Add your build commands here
          # npm install && npm run build
          # or
          # mvn clean package
      
      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          echo "Running tests for: ${{ env.SAFE_PR_TITLE }}"
          # Add your test commands here
          # npm test
      
      - name: Create build artifact with PR info
        run: |
          mkdir -p build-info
          cat > build-info/pr-metadata.json <<EOF
          {
            "pr_number": ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }},
            "pr_title": $(jq -n --arg title "${{ env.SAFE_PR_TITLE }}" '$title'),
            "pr_author": $(jq -n --arg author "${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}" '$author'),
            "commit_sha": "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}",
            "build_time": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
          }
          EOF
          cat build-info/pr-metadata.json
      
      - name: Send Slack notification
        if: always()
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          if [ -n "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
            STATUS="${{ job.status }}"
            COLOR="good"
            if [ "$STATUS" != "success" ]; then
              COLOR="danger"
            fi
            
            PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
              --arg title "${{ env.SAFE_PR_TITLE }}" \
              --arg body "${{ env.SAFE_PR_BODY }}" \
              --arg status "$STATUS" \
              --arg color "$COLOR" \
              --arg pr_number "${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}" \
              --arg pr_url "${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}" \
              --arg author "${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}" \
              '{
                "attachments": [{
                  "color": $color,
                  "title": ("PR #" + $pr_number + ": " + $title),
                  "title_link": $pr_url,
                  "fields": [
                    {
                      "title": "Status",
                      "value": $status,
                      "short": true
                    },
                    {
                      "title": "Author",
                      "value": $author,
                      "short": true
                    },
                    {
                      "title": "Description",
                      "value": $body,
                      "short": false
                    }
                  ]
                }]
              }')
            
            curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
              --data "$PAYLOAD" \
              "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL"
          fi
      
      - name: Send email notification
        if: always()
        env:
          NOTIFICATION_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_EMAIL }}
        run: |
          if [ -n "$NOTIFICATION_EMAIL" ]; then
            cat > email-body.txt <<EOF
          Build Status: ${{ job.status }}
          PR Number: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          PR Title: ${{ env.SAFE_PR_TITLE }}
          Author: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}
          
          Description:
          ${{ env.SAFE_PR_BODY }}
          
          PR URL: ${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}
          EOF
            
            echo "Email notification prepared (configure your email service)"
            cat email-body.txt
          fi
      
      - name: Comment on PR
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const status = '${{ job.status }}';
            const emoji = status === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
            
            const body = `${emoji} Build ${status}
            
            **PR Title:** ${{ env.SAFE_PR_TITLE }}
            **Commit:** \`${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\`
            **Build Time:** ${new Date().toISOString()}
            
            This build information will be included in release notes.`;
            
            await github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              body: body
            });
      
      - name: Upload build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}-build-info
          path: build-info/
          retention-days: 30
